segments.text: This new tool can now predict which
parts of your DNA might cause serious
diseases which could save your life. We
have no idea what most of the DNA in our
body actually does. We do know that just
2% of our DNA is responsible for making
proteins which do most of the work in
our cells. But this other 98% remained a
mystery for decades. Scientists
literally called it junk DNA. But now we
know it's key to regulating that 2%
which relates to some diseases and
mutations throughout it can lead to
others. So to actually understand these
effects which could have a huge impact
on our health, scientists are turning to
this AI model which can analyze up to a
million base pairs at a time and
essentially predict if you make a change
to this letter here would that be
harmful and cause disease or would it do
nothing? I spoke to the head of the team
that made this and he gave me some
examples of what we could do if we could
change those sections. to see that.
Subscribe.
